statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n representing the number of items. The next n lines contain two space-separated integers - the weights and values of the items. The last line contains an integer k representing the capacity of the knapsack.
statement.outputFormat: Output the maximum value of the items that can be put in the knapsack.

examples.explanation: The maximum value is obtained by including the first and third items.
The maximum value is obtained by including the second item.

hints.text: Review the memoization table to ensure it's correctly storing subproblems.
Consider how the items are being processed in the dynamic programming loop.

editorial.title: How to Fix Incorrect Memoization in Knapsack Problem
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to correctly store and retrieve the subproblems in the memoization table.
editorial.bruteForce: A naive approach would involve recursively trying all possible combinations of items and their values.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach would utilize dynamic programming to store the solutions to subproblems.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves iterating over the items and their weights, and for each item, deciding whether to include it in the knapsack or not. This decision is made based on the maximum value we can get with the remaining capacity of the knapsack.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that it always chooses the best option for each item, given the remaining capacity of the knapsack.
editorial.dryRun: Let's say we have items with values 10, 20, and 30, and a knapsack capacity of 8. We want to fill the knapsack with the maximum value. In this case, we would include the first two items (10 and 20) and get a total value of 30.
commonMistakes: Not properly considering the remaining capacity of the knapsack

functionSignature.cpp: int knapsack(vector<vector<int>>& dp, int i, int w, vector<pair<int, int>>& items);
functionSignature.java: int knapsack(int[][] dp, int i, int w, int[][] items);
functionSignature.python: def knapsack(self, dp, i, w, items: list[list[int]]): -> int
functionSignature.javascript: var knapsack = function(dp, i, w, items){
